evidence_items.text: NVIDIA vs AMD (2025): GPUs, AI & Market Share
The multibillion-dollar deal is AMD's latest move to catch up to Nvidia in the lucrative world of selling artificial intelligence chips.
Nvidia dominates gaming GPU market with 95 percent share as sales of AMD Radeon graphics plummet to a historical low of 5 percent | Tom's Hardware
Nvidia: The AI Supercycle Is Big Enough for Both.

findings.text: Nvidia holds a 95 percent share of the gaming GPU market, while AMD Radeon graphics sales have reached a historical low of 5 percent.
AMD has entered into a multibillion-dollar 'chips-for-stock' deal as part of an effort to catch up to Nvidia in the AI chip market.
Some perspectives suggest that the 'AI Supercycle' is large enough to accommodate both AMD and Nvidia.

inferences: AMD is leveraging strategic financial partnerships (such as the chips-for-stock deal) to offset Nvidia's current market dominance in AI hardware.
